steps.title: Graph both sides first
steps.content:
A fast Desmos method is to graph the left and right sides separately:
$$y=2x^2-2$$
$$y=2x+3$$
The x-coordinates of the intersections are the solutions.

steps.title: Match the exact solution
steps.content: The positive intersection is approximately $2.158$. Among the choices, $\frac{1+\sqrt{11}}{2}\approx2.158$, so choice D (‘$\frac{1 + \sqrt{11}}{2}$’) matches the graph.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:
$$2x^2-2x-5=0$$
Quadratic formula:
$$x=\frac{2\pm\sqrt{44}}{4}=\frac{1\pm\sqrt{11}}{2}$$
Choice D (‘$\frac{1 + \sqrt{11}}{2}$’) is one of these solutions, so D is correct.
